$description: Privacy and security research for products with cameras or internet access. Hand-curated from manufacturer pages, manufacturer privacy policies, and credible third-party reviews. Keys are product slugs from components/site-data.ts. The generate-products script merges this into site-data.ts under each product's 'privacy' field.
$sourceRules: Only research findings from primary sources (manufacturer pages, manufacturer privacy policies) or credible third-party reviewers.
Each 'value' is from a controlled vocabulary appropriate to the field. 'note' adds nuance.
Add a 'lastResearched' date and at least one URL in 'sources' for every product so anyone can re-verify.
If a product no longer applies (no longer has a camera), remove its entry from this file.

privacyShutter.note: One-tap privacy mode disables camera and microphone in software. No physical lens cover.

indicatorLED.note: Not explicitly documented in available materials.

twoFactorAuth.note: Not advertised in the EBO companion app description or the privacy policy.

privacyPolicyClarity.note: Policy exists and explicitly states that cloud video storage is opt-in. Some sections are generic boilerplate.

storageLocation.note: 32GB onboard storage included, expandable to 256GB via microSD. Cloud video storage is opt-in.

knownIncidents.note: No major public security incidents found as of research date.

ebo-air-2-familybot.summary: Local-first storage is a positive. Software-only privacy mode and lack of documented 2FA are weaknesses. A reasonable choice for buyers who want the home-monitoring features and are comfortable managing app security themselves.

privacyShutter.note: Inherits Enabot's one-tap software privacy mode. No physical lens cover.

twoFactorAuth.note: Not advertised in companion app description or privacy policy.

privacyPolicyClarity.note: Same policy as other Enabot products. Cloud video storage is opt-in.

storageLocation.note: 32GB onboard storage with 256GB microSD support. Multi-user access with per-account permissions.

ebo-air-2s-familybot.summary: Same privacy profile as the EBO Air 2. Local-first storage with opt-in cloud is the most positive aspect. Software-only privacy mode is the main gap.

privacyShutter.note: One-tap privacy mode toggles camera and microphone in software. No physical lens cover.

twoFactorAuth.note: Not advertised in companion app description or privacy policy.

privacyPolicyClarity.note: Same policy as other Enabot products. Cloud video storage is opt-in.

storageLocation.note: 32GB onboard storage with microSD support. Cloud is opt-in.

ebo-air-2-plus-familybot.summary: Adds AI Chat Mode on top of the Air 2 base. AI conversations route through cloud services when enabled, so the privacy considerations are slightly heavier than the base Air 2. Other aspects are equivalent.

privacyShutter.note: One-tap privacy mode. No physical lens cover.

privacyPolicyClarity.note: Same Enabot policy. Cloud features for ChatGPT/Alexa integration imply additional third-party data flow when enabled.

storageLocation.note: Local storage for video by default. ChatGPT and Alexa integrations route through their respective cloud services when used.

ebo-x-familybot.summary: Top of Enabot's line with 4K video, facial recognition, and ChatGPT/Alexa integration. The additional AI features depend on third-party cloud services (OpenAI, Amazon). Buyers comfortable with those companies' data practices will find the additional capability worthwhile; those who aren't should consider a lighter-feature model.

privacyShutter.note: Software-only camera disable via the ROLA app. No physical lens cover.

privacyPolicyClarity.note: ROLA-specific privacy policy exists. Cloud video storage is opt-in.

storageLocation.note: Local-first storage; cloud requires manual activation.

rola-mini-pet-monitor.summary: Pet-focused variant of the Enabot platform with a treat dispenser. Same privacy profile as the EBO line: local-first, opt-in cloud, software privacy controls.

privacyShutter.note: Camera is described as 'near-invisible' and embedded in the nose. No documented physical cover; also no visible camera, which is a transparency trade-off.

privacyPolicyClarity.note: Manufacturer documents the privacy model clearly: optional cloud features (Dream Sketch AI Art) must be manually enabled, photos are encrypted before leaving the device, daily interactions use a local offline AI model and upload no data.

storageLocation.note: Daily interactions are processed locally with no cloud upload. Optional Dream Sketch AI Art uploads encrypted images only when the user explicitly invokes the feature.

knownIncidents.note: New product introduced at CES 2025; no public incidents yet.

kamomo.summary: Best-in-class privacy profile for daily interaction in this catalog: local-only AI processing for everyday use, opt-in encrypted cloud for the specific Dream Sketch feature. The lack of a physical camera shutter is the main residual gap; the camera is also not visibly placed, which some buyers prefer and others find concerning.

privacyPolicyClarity.note: Detailed privacy policy with regional data storage commitments (EU data stored in EU, China data in China), children-under-16 protections requiring guardian consent, and explicit account deletion process. Among the more detailed in the category.

storageLocation.note: Face data, body data, and all other camera perception data are processed locally only. Voice analysis uses AWS Lex with real-time analysis and no storage. Remote monitoring uses AWS KVS with hardware encryption.

knownIncidents.note: No public incidents found. Multiple awards including CES Innovation Awards 2024 and iF Design Awards 2024.

robot-pet-dog.summary: Strong technical privacy profile. Local processing for camera perception is best-in-class for the category. Cloud component (remote monitoring, voice) uses major-provider infrastructure with industry-standard compliance certifications. Main gap is the lack of a physical camera shutter.
